intro: Damrongia kerrii, also known as Thai Krachaidao, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, traditionally classified as a type of ginger, although it belongs to the family Zingiberaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Damrongia kerrii include terpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into various topical preparations such as creams and ointments.
